title: Oral and Dental Health Program Educational Objectives
content:
Oral and Dental Health Program Educational Objectives

Program Educational Objectives

1. To train qualified health technicians who have basic knowledge and skills in the field, who can assist the physician in clinical processes in dentist offices, public or private dental polyclinics.

2. To gain individuals who will fulfil their professional responsibilities in accordance with laws, regulations, legislation, ethical principles and human rights, and who can take responsibility for patient acceptance, treatment processes and records.

3. To educate graduates who adopt a lifelong learning approach, participate in scientific activities and are equipped to respond to the changing needs of patients.

Objectives

1. Professional Knowledge and Application Competence

To train health technicians who have basic knowledge and skills in the field of oral and dental health; who can use the materials, equipment and technologies used in the clinical applications of the dentist effectively and safely.

2. Ethics, Communication and Teamwork Skills

To provide graduates who act in accordance with patient rights, professional ethical rules, laws and legislation; who can communicate effectively with dentists, health team, patients and their relatives; who can provide quality service by taking responsibility within the team.

3. Continuous Professional Development and Adaptation to Innovations

To train professionals who adopt lifelong learning, follow scientific developments and technological innovations, contribute to the protection of individual and community health, and are sensitive to the environment and occupational health and safety rules.
